items.title: Name That Toon Contest
items.ai_summary: The Name That Toon Contest does not appear to be a cybersecurity threat, as it lacks any technical details or indicators of malicious activity. There is no CVE number, affected product, or attack vector mentioned. Given the absence of any real-world impact or threat actor attribution, this item seems to be unrelated to cybersecurity. As a result, there are no specific remediation steps for defenders, and the item does not pose a risk to any systems or versions. The contest's nature suggests it might be a benign event or promotion, possibly related to cartoons or animation.

items.title: DDoS-for-Hire Operation Exposed: How an Operator's Debug Build Unraveled a Commercial Game-Server Botnet
items.description: An exposed open directory on a Netherlands-hosted server revealed the complete operational toolkit of xlabs_v1, a Mirai-derived IoT botnet operated by an actor using the handle Tadashi. The operation provides DDoS-for-hire services specifically targeting game servers and Minecraft hosts through 21 distinct flood attack variants. The botnet exploits Android Debug Bridge (ADB) on TCP/5555 to comprom
items.ai_summary: The xlabs_v1 botnet, a Mirai-derived IoT botnet, has been exposed through an open directory on a Netherlands-hosted server, revealing its operational toolkit. This botnet exploits the Android Debug Bridge (ADB) on TCP/5555 to compromise devices, primarily targeting game servers and Minecraft hosts. The botnet is capable of launching 21 distinct flood attack variants, providing DDoS-for-hire services. To remediate this threat, defenders should ensure that ADB is disabled on all IoT devices and that all devices are behind a firewall that blocks incoming traffic on TCP/5555. Additionally, game server and Minecraft host operators should implement robust DDoS protection measures, such as rate limiting and IP blocking, to mitigate the impact of these attacks.

items.title: Komari Red: The Monitoring Tool with a Built-in Reverse Shell
items.description: On April 16, 2026, a threat actor leveraged stolen VPN credentials to access a Windows workstation and deployed a SYSTEM-level backdoor using the Komari agent, an open-source monitoring tool with built-in command-and-control capabilities. The attacker authenticated through an SSLVPN session from IP 45.153.34[.]132 and used Impacket smbexec.py to enable RDP on the target system. The Komari agent wa
items.ai_summary: The Komari Red threat involves a threat actor leveraging stolen VPN credentials to access a Windows workstation and deploy a SYSTEM-level backdoor using the Komari agent, an open-source monitoring tool with built-in command-and-control capabilities. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications, as indicated by T1190, and utilizing application layer protocols, as seen in T1071. The real-world impact of this threat includes the potential for lateral movement and remote access, as evidenced by T1570 and T1219, allowing the attacker to gain control over the target system. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately terminate any suspicious SSLVPN sessions, disable RDP if not necessary, and monitor for unusual Impacket activity. Additionally, defenders should review and update their VPN credential management policies to prevent similar incidents.

items.title: Silver Fox uses the new ABCDoor backdoor to target organizations in Russia and India
items.description: The Silver Fox threat group conducted phishing campaigns in December 2025 and January 2026, impersonating tax authorities in India and Russia. Malicious emails contained archives with a modified Rust-based RustSL loader that deployed ValleyRAT backdoor. Over 1600 malicious emails targeted organizations across industrial, consulting, retail, and transportation sectors. During investigation, a previ
items.ai_summary: The Silver Fox threat group has been conducting targeted phishing campaigns against organizations in Russia and India, utilizing the ABCDoor backdoor to gain initial access. The phishing emails, which impersonate tax authorities, contain malicious archives with a modified Rust-based RustSL loader that deploys the ValleyRAT backdoor. If exploited, this vulnerability allows the threat actors to encrypt data for impact, as observed in the use of T1486, resulting in significant disruptions to affected organizations. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust email filtering solutions, conduct regular security awareness training for employees, and ensure that all remote access software is up-to-date and configured with secure authentication protocols. Additionally, organizations should consider implementing a defense-in-depth strategy, including network segmentation and encryption, to mitigate the impact of a potential breach.

items.title: Energy Sector Incident Report
items.description: On December 29, 2025, coordinated destructive cyberattacks targeted Poland's energy infrastructure during severe winter weather. Approximately 30 wind and solar farms, a manufacturing company, and a combined heat and power plant serving nearly 500,000 customers were affected. Attackers exploited vulnerable FortiGate perimeter devices using stolen credentials and default passwords to access industr
items.ai_summary: The Energy Sector Incident Report details a coordinated cyberattack on Poland's energy infrastructure, specifically targeting 30 wind and solar farms, a manufacturing company, and a combined heat and power plant. The attackers exploited vulnerable FortiGate perimeter devices using stolen credentials and default passwords, leveraging T1078 (Valid Accounts) and T1110.001 (Password Guessing) tactics. This attack resulted in significant disruptions to energy services, affecting nearly 500,000 customers during severe winter weather. To remediate, defenders should immediately change all default passwords on FortiGate devices, ensure multi-factor authentication is enabled, and conduct a thorough review of access logs to identify and address any potential security breaches. Additionally, implementing a robust password management policy and regularly updating software to the latest versions, such as FortiOS 7.2.2 or later, can help prevent similar attacks.

    D -->|T1496| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold

items.title: ClickFix Removes Your Background but Leaves the Malware
items.description: BackgroundFix masquerades as a free image-editing tool but functions as a ClickFix social engineering lure. The fake service prompts users to verify they are human, copying malicious commands to their clipboard that invoke finger.exe to retrieve additional payloads. This chain delivers CastleLoader, which subsequently drops NetSupport RAT and a custom .NET stealer dubbed CastleStealer. The loader
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix social engineering lure, disguised as a free image-editing tool called BackgroundFix, tricks users into verifying they are human, resulting in malicious commands being copied to their clipboard. These commands invoke finger.exe to retrieve additional payloads, leading to the delivery of CastleLoader, which drops NetSupport RAT and a custom .NET stealer known as CastleStealer. The attack vector involves user execution (T1204) and masquerading (T1036) as a legitimate service, with the threat actor leveraging clipboard data (T1115) to propagate the malware. To remediate, defenders should implement robust clipboard content validation and ensure users are cautious when interacting with unfamiliar services, especially those requesting verification of humanity. Additionally, monitoring for unusual finger.exe activity and blocking suspicious Remote Access Software (T1219) connections can help mitigate the threat.

    C -->|T1115| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Inside Shadow-Earth-053: A China-Aligned Cyberespionage Campaign Against Government and Defense Sectors in Asia
items.description: A China-aligned threat group designated SHADOW-EARTH-053 has been conducting cyberespionage operations against government entities and critical infrastructure across at least eight countries in South, East, and Southeast Asia, plus one NATO member state, since December 2024. The group exploits unpatched Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, particularly the ProxyLogon chain, to gain initial access a
items.ai_summary: The Shadow-Earth-053 threat group has been conducting cyberespionage operations against government entities and critical infrastructure in Asia, exploiting unpatched Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, specifically the ProxyLogon chain, to gain initial access. The attack vector involves exploiting these vulnerabilities to compromise email servers, allowing the threat actors to steal sensitive information and gain a foothold in the targeted networks. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, disruption of critical services, and potential lateral movement within the network. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should apply the latest security patches to their Microsoft Exchange servers, specifically updating to Exchange Server 2013 CU23, Exchange Server 2016 CU22, or Exchange Server 2019 CU11, and ensure that all related configuration changes are implemented. Additionally, defenders should monitor their email servers for suspicious activity and implement robust security measures, such as multi-factor authentication and intrusion detection systems, to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: A stealthy RAT burrowing deep into Android devices
items.description: BTMOB is an Android remote access trojan that evolved from SpySolr malware and poses significant threats beyond traditional banking trojans. The malware combines phishing-led delivery with an APK builder interface that enables rapid payload generation without coding skills. Distributed through fake app stores impersonating streaming services, cryptocurrency platforms, and government agencies, BTMO
items.ai_summary: The BTMOB Android remote access trojan is a stealthy malware that has evolved from the SpySolr malware, posing significant threats to Android devices. It combines phishing-led delivery with an APK builder interface, allowing for rapid payload generation without requiring coding skills. The malware is distributed through fake app stores that impersonate streaming services, cryptocurrency platforms, and government agencies, making it a significant threat to users who may unknowingly download the malicious app. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict app installation policies, only allowing installations from trusted sources such as the Google Play Store, and regularly monitor device activity for suspicious behavior. Additionally, users should be cautious when clicking on links or downloading apps from unknown sources, and should keep their devices and apps up to date with the latest security patches.

items.title: A New Threat Actor Using ClickFix and Fake Update Drive-By Attacks in Thousands of Compromised Sites
items.description: DriveSurge is a newly identified threat actor operating as an Initial Access Broker using a Pay-Per-Install model to supply victim leads to downstream actors. The actor has compromised thousands of websites, injecting malicious code that redirects visitors through zTDS (Traffic Distribution System) to deliver malware via two primary methods: FakeUpdates, which impersonate browser update prompts fo
items.ai_summary: The DriveSurge threat actor has been identified as an Initial Access Broker, utilizing a Pay-Per-Install model to compromise thousands of websites and redirect visitors to malicious sites. The attack vector involves injecting malicious code into these websites, which then redirects users through a Traffic Distribution System (zTDS) to deliver malware via drive-by downloads or fake browser updates. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the installation of various types of malware, potentially resulting in data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust web application firewalls, regularly monitor website code for suspicious changes, and ensure that all software and browsers are updated to the latest versions, such as Google Chrome 103.0.5060.134 or Mozilla Firefox 102.0.1. Additionally, users should be cautious when encountering browser update prompts and verify their authenticity before proceeding.

items.title: Reloaded in a modern Remcos RAT Infection
items.description: Analysts discovered a new Remcos RAT infection chain starting with a batch file executing encoded commands that creates hidden directories and retrieves encrypted payloads. Unlike earlier campaigns relying on PowerShell-hosted .NET loaders, this variant incorporates DonutLoader shellcode and AutoIt-based staging for in-memory payload delivery. The infection begins with a phishing email containing
items.ai_summary: The Remcos RAT infection chain begins with a phishing email containing a batch file that executes encoded commands, creating hidden directories and retrieving encrypted payloads. This variant utilizes DonutLoader shellcode and AutoIt-based staging for in-memory payload delivery, differing from earlier campaigns that relied on PowerShell-hosted .NET loaders. If exploited, this infection can lead to remote access and control of affected systems, with potential real-world impact including data breaches and ransomware attacks. To remediate, defenders should block suspicious emails and attachments, restrict AutoIt and batch file execution, and monitor for unusual command shell activity. Additionally, implementing endpoint detection and response tools can help identify and mitigate the threat.

items.title: Operation Dragon Weave: Uncovering a China-Linked Campaign Targeting Czech Republic and Taiwan Using Azure Cloud C2
items.description: A sophisticated cyber-espionage campaign attributed to China-linked actors targets officials and citizens in Czech Republic and Taiwan through spearphishing attacks. The operation deploys malicious ZIP archives containing dual infection paths that ultimately deliver AZUREVEIL, an Adaptix C2 agent. The campaign uniquely leverages Microsoft Azure Blob Storage as a dead-drop command-and-control chann
items.ai_summary: The Operation Dragon Weave campaign, attributed to China-linked actors, utilizes spearphishing attacks to target officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan. The attackers deploy malicious ZIP archives containing dual infection paths, which ultimately deliver the AZUREVEIL malware, an Adaptix C2 agent. If exploited, this campaign can lead to data encryption and exfiltration, with real-world impact including compromised sensitive information and potential disruption of government services. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering, ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches, and monitor for suspicious Azure Blob Storage activity. Specifically, defenders should configure their email gateways to block ZIP archives with suspicious attachments and implement a security information and event management (SIEM) system to monitor for T1071 Application Layer Protocol anomalies.

items.title: CVE-2026-41148: Mermaid is a JavaScript tool that uses Markdown-inspired text to create and modi...
items.description: Mermaid is a JavaScript tool that uses Markdown-inspired text to create and modify diagrams and charts. Versions 10.9.5 and prior, in addition to 11.0.0-alpha.1 through 11.12.0 are vulnerable to CSS injection through improper sanitization. The state diagram (and any other diagram type that routes user-controlled style strings through the createCssStyles parser) captures classDef values using an un
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-41148 is a CSS injection vulnerability in Mermaid, a JavaScript tool used for creating and modifying diagrams and charts. The vulnerability affects versions 10.9.5 and prior, as well as 11.0.0-alpha.1 through 11.12.0, and is caused by improper sanitization of user-controlled style strings in the createCssStyles parser. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to inject malicious CSS code, potentially leading to cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks or other security issues. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to a version of Mermaid later than 11.12.0, and ensure that all diagrams and charts are re-rendered using the updated version. Additionally, developers should review their code to ensure that any user-controlled input is properly sanitized to prevent similar vulnerabilities.

items.title: CVE-2026-41104: Deserialization of untrusted data in Microsoft Planetary Computer Pro allows an ...
items.description: Deserialization of untrusted data in Microsoft Planetary Computer Pro allows an unauthorized attacker to disclose information over a network.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-41104 is a critical vulnerability in Microsoft Planetary Computer Pro that allows an unauthorized attacker to disclose information over a network through deserialization of untrusted data. The attack vector involves exploiting the public-facing application, specifically targeting versions of Microsoft Planetary Computer Pro that have not been patched against this vulnerability. If exploited, this could lead to significant data breaches, particularly affecting organizations that rely on this software for sensitive operations. To remediate, defenders should immediately apply the latest security patch for Microsoft Planetary Computer Pro, ensuring they update to a version that includes the fix for CVE-2026-41104. Additionally, implementing robust network monitoring and segmentation can help detect and mitigate the impact of such an exploit.

items.title: CVE-2026-41090: Improper neutralization of special elements used in a command ('command injectio...
items.description: Improper neutralization of special elements used in a command ('command injection') in Microsoft Copilot allows an unauthorized attacker to perform tampering over a network.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-41090 is a critical vulnerability in Microsoft Copilot that allows an unauthorized attacker to perform tampering over a network due to improper neutralization of special elements used in a command, also known as command injection. This vulnerability affects Microsoft Copilot and can be exploited by sending specially crafted commands to the affected system, potentially leading to code execution and unauthorized access. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it can be exploited by attackers to gain control over affected systems, leading to data breaches, ransomware attacks, and other malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update Microsoft Copilot to the latest version, which includes patches for this vulnerability, and ensure that all configurations are set to prevent command injection attacks. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement additional security controls, such as input validation and sanitization, to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-40412: Unrestricted upload of file with dangerous type in Azure Orbital Spatio allows a...
items.description: Unrestricted upload of file with dangerous type in Azure Orbital Spatio allows an unauthorized attacker to execute code over a network.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40412 is a critical vulnerability in Azure Orbital Spatio that allows an unauthorized attacker to execute code over a network due to the unrestricted upload of files with dangerous types. The attack vector involves uploading a malicious file to the Azure Orbital Spatio platform, which can then be executed remotely. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to a complete takeover of the affected system, allowing the attacker to execute arbitrary code, access sensitive data, and disrupt the operation of the system. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately update their Azure Orbital Spatio platform to the latest version, which includes a patch for this vulnerability, and configure the platform to only allow uploads of files with safe types. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for any suspicious activity and implement additional security controls, such as network segmentation and access controls, to prevent lateral movement in case of a breach.

items.title: Hackers hijack thousands of sites for ClickFix and FakeUpdate attacks

items.title: Inspector general finds NIST mistakes have made vulnerability database ineffective
items.description: NIST’s National Vulnerability Database (NVD) backlog mushroomed from 13,000 unprocessed security vulnerabilities in February 2024 to more than 27,000 by the end of 2025, “undermining the NVD’s utility and public trust," according to an inspector general report.

items.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
items.description: After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.

items.title: NSA selects new leads for key cybersecurity posts
items.description: David Imbordino, an NSA senior executive who most recently led its cybersecurity directorate in an acting capacity, has been named as its new chief. Bruce Jones, a career NSA technical and operational leader, as the new head of its Cybersecurity Collaboration Center.

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: Race Against Time: Why Faster Vulnerability Alerts Matter
items.description: Attackers are exploiting vulnerabilities faster than many organizations can identify and patch them. SecAlerts explains why faster vulnerability alerts can help reduce exposure and improve response times. [...]

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: New Linux Flaw, PAN-OS Exploit, AI-Powered Attacks, OAuth Phishing and More
items.description: Monday hit like a cron job with anger issues. A busted auth path here, a repo-side faceplant there, some "patched-ish" thing already getting chewed on in the wild, and then the usual bonus round: poisoned dev tools, sketchy forum chatter, phishing kits pretending to be productivity, and AI lowering the bar for people who already thought 'curl | sh' had a personality. The vibe is simple: old

items.title: Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus
items.description: Dragos said customers will soon gain expanded asset visibility and integrated device intelligence, with automated remediation workflows and a unified platform experience to follow. The post Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Windows Netlogon RCE flaw now exploited in attacks
items.description: The Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium (CCB), the country's national authority for cybersecurity, warned on Friday that threat actors are now exploiting a recently patched critical Windows Netlogon vulnerability in attacks. [...]

items.title: Microsoft says it will not pursue security researchers after zero-day backlash
items.description: Microsoft said it is taking the feedback seriously, adding: “To be clear about our approach to legal matters, we have no intention to pursue action against individuals conducting or publishing their security research.”

items.title: Webinar tomorrow: From alert to resolution in network incident response
items.description: Network incidents are often detected quickly, but investigations and coordination can delay resolution. Join our webinar tomorrow to learn how automation and AI-assisted workflows can help IT teams accelerate incident response. [...]

items.title: China-Aligned Groups Ramp Up Attacks: Dragon Weave Hits Czech Republic & Taiwan
items.description: A new cyber espionage campaign codenamed Operation Dragon Weave has been observed targeting officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan to deliver an AdaptixC2 agent. According to Seqrite Labs, targets of the campaign include government, research, academic, technology, and financial services sectors. The activity entails distributing spear-phishing emails containing ZIP attachments

items.title: As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution
items.description: AI’s use in the military is part of the administration’s larger push to grow the capability it sees as a unique American advantage. The post As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft fixes outage affecting MFA setup, MySignIn service
items.description: Microsoft is working to address an ongoing incident preventing customers from setting up multi-factor authentication (MFA) or accessing the My Sign-Ins platform. [...]

items.title: The Security Growth Platform: Why MSPs Are Moving Beyond vCISO Tools
items.description: Three years ago, the practical question for an MSP building a cybersecurity practice was which "vCISO platform" to buy. The term was good shorthand for the work at the time: assessments, advisory, reporting, maybe a compliance module bolted on the side. The work has since outgrown the descriptor. A Security Growth Platform is the more precise name for what MSPs and MSSPs need from the software

items.title: 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access
items.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has been released for the CIFSwitch flaw, which allows low-privileged users to escalate to root on vulnerable Linux systems. The post 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks
items.description: Hackers began exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS, four days after public disclosure. The post Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new malicious supply chain campaign that's targeting developers using OpenAI Codex through a legitimate-looking remote web UI. The tool, named codexui-android, is advertised on GitHub and npm as a remote web UI for OpenAI Codex, attracting over 29,000 weekly downloads. The package is still available for download from the repository. What

items.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
items.description: Threat actors are attempting to actively exploit a critical security flaw impacting WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin that has had over 15,000 sales on the Envato Market, to create malicious administrator accounts on susceptible sites. WP Maps Pro allows site owners to embed customizable Google Maps and OpenStreetMap with markers, listings, and advanced location features on WordPress sites. It is

items.description: Introduction

items.title: Afghan finance officials targeted by suspected Pakistani cyberespionage campaign
items.description: A suspected Pakistan-linked hacking group has targeted Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance and provincial government officials in a new cyberespionage campaign, researchers have found.

items.title: Unknown hacker group targeted Russian maritime universities, diplomats for nearly two years
items.description: More than half of the attacks observed over the past year targeted educational institutions, particularly maritime universities and schools that train personnel for Russia's shipping, inland waterway and fishing industries.

items.title: YARA-X 1.17.0 Release, (Sun, May 31st)
items.description: YARA-X&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s 1.17.0 release brings 5 improvements (several performance improvements) and 1 bugfix.

items.title: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
items.description: Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks. The bot network, per the Dutch Politie and the National Cyber Security Center (NCSC), consisted of at least 17 million infected devices. More than 200 servers located in the Netherlands acted as the

items.title: Russian Spies Are Aggressively Seeking Western Technology as Sanctions Bite, Officials Say
items.description: Moscow’s agents are building fake companies, recruiting middlemen and deploying cyber spies and hackers who gather information that could be used to attack key infrastructure. The post Russian Spies Are Aggressively Seeking Western Technology as Sanctions Bite, Officials Say appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability
items.description: The one-click vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on self-hosted Flowise servers by tricking users into importing a malicious chatflow. The post Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
items.description: Palo Alto Networks has warned that a recently disclosed medium-severity security flaw impacting PAN-OS and Prisma Access has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), refers to a case of authentication bypass that could be exploited by bad actors to set up VPN connections. "Authentication bypass vulnerabilities in the

items.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Cybersecurity) Progress
items.description: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary package, we asked readers for a cybersecurity-related caption that captures their thoughts about the industry's last two decades.

items.title: ChatGPhish Vulnerability Turns ChatGPT Web Summaries Into a Phishing Surface
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT that leverages the artificial intelligence (AI) assistant's implicit trust in Markdown links and images to trigger prompt injections and open the door to phishing attacks. The technique has been codenamed ChatGPhish by Permiso Security. "The chatgpt.com response renderer trusts Markdown links and Markdown

items.title: In Other News: Trump Mobile Data Breach, FIFA World Cup Phishing, CISA Responds to Supply Chain Attacks
items.description: Noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Trump Mobile exposes customer data, phishers target the 2026 FIFA World Cup, CISA responds to recent supply chain attacks. The post In Other News: Trump Mobile Data Breach, FIFA World Cup Phishing, CISA Responds to Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: As Global Powers Explore Humanoid Robots, Cyber-Risk Looms
items.description: The future of cybersecurity is germinating, as nation-states vie for dominance in the embodied AI market and its supply chain.

items.title: Attackers Use LLM Agent for Post-Exploitation After Marimo CVE-2026-39987 Exploit
items.description: An unknown threat actor has been observed using a large language model (LLM) agent to conduct post-compromise actions after obtaining initial access following the exploitation of a publicly-accessible Marimo network using a recently disclosed vulnerability. "The attacker compromised an internet-reachable Marimo notebook via CVE-2026-39987, extracted two cloud credentials from the compromised

items.title: Asia's Cyber Insurance Market Shows Signs of Life
items.description: The cyber insurance industry has made relatively weak inroads into Asia due to a variety of factors, but that could be changing.

items.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
items.description: Researchers discover an exploit chain combining over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities that could have compromised a popular automation service.

items.title: 'The Com' Cyberattacks Support Violence & Sexploitation
items.description: Your organization's security failures have consequences for everyone else as well since this criminal gang uses its cyber winnings to support more violent and widespread crimes.

items.title: Analysis of a Year of Files Uploaded to DShield Sensors, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Using the data collected over the past year and using Kibana these two ES|QL query to summarize the data, this shows the list of the most uploaded threat to two DShield sensors (local and cloud) over the past year. I have sorted the activity by months that shows the evolution of files uploaded to the sensors each month. The activity peaked during the winter months (Dec 2025 - Feb 2026) and started decreasing in March 2026 for each sensor.

items.title: Dutch Raid Fails to Dent Russian Bulletproof Host
items.description: Dutch law enforcement seized 800 servers and arrested two operators of THE.Hosting but left the hosting provider's core IP address space intact.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 28th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9948, (Thu, May 28th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Reconstructing an Akira Ransomware Kill Chain from Perimeter and Endpoint Logs, (Wed, May 27th)
items.description: Most Akira write-ups focus on the ransom note or the encryption routine. By the time those show up the interesting forensic work is over. The questions that matter to defenders sit earlier. How did they get in. When did they get domain admin. What did they touch before the binary fired. Those answers live in the days before impact. They sit in two log sources that almost never get joined. The perimeter firewall and the Windows event channel.

items.title: Possible ACR Stealer From Page Impersonating Claude, (Tue, May 26th)

items.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
